figure.title: UALink TL Flow Control Conceptual Diagram
figure.purpose: Shows where flow control (FC) mechanisms are applied within the UALink Transaction Layer.

technical_summary: Uses the same base conceptual diagram as the general UALink TL (Figure 5-a).
A yellow block labeled 'FC' (Flow Control) is added.
The FC block monitors/controls the inbound and outbound traffic paths.
Arrows from the FC block point to the UPLI Completer's outbound response channels (Rd Rsp/Data, Wr Rsp) and the UPLI Completer's inbound request channels (Req, OrigData).
This indicates that flow control heavily manages traffic destined for the external upper layers and outgoing from them.

components.name: FC
components.description: Flow Control block managing credit and backpressure.

critical_facts: Flow control in the UALink TL intercepts and regulates traffic flows between the TL internals and the UPLI interfaces to prevent buffer overflow.
